results.description: Every material in scene.materials must have component fractions summing to ~ 1.0.
results.name: material_composition_sums_to_one

results.description: Re-running a deterministic scenario with the same seed must produce matching run-end scores (numerically-equal to within ~1e-9 relative; integer / string / boolean fields must be exactly equal). Strict byte-identical replay is *not* required because Geant4's MT accumulators sum doubles in a thread-arrival order that varies by 1 ULP between runs.
results.name: determinism_replay
results.summary: all numeric fields agree within ~1e-9 relative; integers/strings/booleans exact-match

results.description: OnlineEventStats mean(total_edep_mev) must match system_event_edep_mean_mev (same data feeds both).
results.name: event_feature_mean_consistent_with_system_summary

results.description: primaries_transmitted + primaries_absorbed == primaries_emitted (closure invariant).
results.name: primaries_accounting_closure

results.description: system_edep_mev_per_mm3 == total_edep_mev / system_volume_mm3 (when volume > 0).
results.name: system_volume_density_arithmetic

results.description: Osmosis scenario: a semipermeable membrane passes water but excludes the larger solute. Asserts the scenario's validation booleans (dimensional exclusion of the solute holds; a net osmotic water shift toward the solute side is observed) -- guards the membrane/diffusion hook path.
results.name: osmotic_shift_observed

results.description: Pascal's-principle scenario: a hook-driven 2D H2O bath transmits a piston pressure to a sensor wall. In the rigid vessel the wall barely moves (pressure transmitted undiminished -> Pascal holds); in the Hookean-deformable vessel the wall expands and damps the pressure. Asserts the scenario's own validation booleans plus rigid << deformable wall displacement -- guards the fluid/pressure hook path.
results.name: pascal_principle_holds

results.description: Reports whether OnlineEventStats ran with Torch acceleration. This is informational — the Welford fallback is correct either way.
results.name: event_feature_stats_torch_backed_flag

results.description: Every configured nuclear cycle must conserve baryon number and charge in both forward and backward reactions.
results.name: nuclear_cycle_conservation

results.description: For every cycle, |forward.Q + backward.Q| <= 1 MeV (closure under round-trip).
results.name: nuclear_cycle_q_value_closure

results.description: Physical invariant: every derived n is >= 1 (Kramers-Kronig output is bounded below by 1).
results.name: optics_index_above_one

results.description: Strict invariant: n_glass > n_water > n_air.
results.name: optics_index_ordering

results.description: Sanity invariant: KK integration window spans at least three decades of energy (so the dispersion integral covers UV/X-ray resonances).
results.name: optics_kk_integration_window

results.description: Mean refractive index derived for air at the visible band vs handbook.
results.name: optics_n_air_visible
references: CRC Handbook of Chemistry & Physics, dry air @ STP

results.description: Mean refractive index derived for glass slab (SiO2) at the visible band vs handbook.
results.name: optics_n_glass_visible
references: Schott BK7 typical n at 589 nm

results.description: Mean refractive index derived for water at the visible band vs CRC handbook.
results.name: optics_n_water_visible
references: CRC Handbook of Chemistry & Physics, water @ 589 nm

results.description: The H2O brine fluid scenario (a Sputnik-milestone scenario) runs to completion, deposits energy in the brine volume, and closes its primary accounting. Guards the element-component + fail-safe material build that fixed the G4_SODIUM_CHLORIDE SIGSEGV: a regression that crashes the run produces no run-end scores and lands here as a fail.
results.name: h2o_fluid_brine_run_closes
results.summary: total_edep=12.6608 MeV  brine(fluid_bulk)_edep=9.0651 MeV  primaries emitted=50 transmitted=50 absorbed=0 (closes=True)

results.description: trech_viz_scene.json must declare schema == 'trech_viz_scene_v1'.
results.name: viz_scene_schema_v1

results.description: Every recorded trajectory must have at least 2 points (otherwise it isn't a polyline).
results.name: viz_trajectories_min_points

results.description: Recorded viz_segments must equal sum(len(points)) across the trajectories JSONL.
results.name: viz_trajectories_point_count
